questions.question: Why do MNCs set up production units in different countries?

options.B: To reduce production cost and increase profits

questions.explanation: MNCs set up units where labor and other costs are lower to maximize profits.

questions.question: Which of the following is a major reason for globalisation?
questions.explanation: Better transport and communication connect markets globally.

questions.question: How do MNCs spread their production globally?
questions.explanation: MNCs expand through Foreign Direct Investment and partnerships.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes liberalisation?
questions.explanation: Liberalisation reduces government restrictions on trade.

questions.question: What is the main role of WTO?
questions.explanation: WTO sets rules for global trade among countries.

questions.question: How does globalisation benefit consumers?
questions.explanation: Consumers get better quality and more options due to competition.

questions.question: Which is a negative impact of globalisation on small producers?
questions.explanation: Small producers struggle to compete with large MNCs.

questions.question: What is outsourcing?
questions.explanation: Outsourcing means giving work to foreign firms.

questions.question: Why did India adopt liberalisation in 1991?
questions.explanation: India opened its economy to global markets in 1991.

questions.question: Which sector benefited most from outsourcing?
questions.explanation: IT and BPO services grew due to outsourcing.

questions.question: Which of the following is a trade barrier?
questions.explanation: Tariffs increase cost of imports.

questions.question: Which company acquired Indian brands like Thums Up?
questions.explanation: Coca-Cola acquired Indian soft drink brands.

questions.question: Why do MNCs prefer countries with cheap labor?
questions.explanation: Lower labor costs increase profit margins.

questions.question: Which reform helped Indian companies compete globally?
questions.explanation: Liberalisation reduced restrictions and encouraged competition.

questions.question: Which is an example of foreign direct investment?

options.B: Opening a factory in another country

questions.explanation: FDI involves investing in production abroad.

questions.question: How has technology supported globalisation?
questions.explanation: Technology speeds up communication and trade.

questions.question: Which statement is true about globalisation?

options.B: It connects producers to global markets

questions.explanation: Globalisation links producers to international markets.

questions.question: Which group benefited most from globalisation in India?
questions.explanation: Large producers benefited from global markets.

questions.question: What happens when import duties are reduced?
questions.explanation: Lower duties make imported goods cheaper.

questions.question: Which factor attracts MNC investment?
questions.explanation: Skilled workforce and infrastructure attract MNCs.

questions.question: Globalisation increases economic:
questions.explanation: Countries become interdependent through trade.

questions.question: Which Indian reform reduced import restrictions?
questions.explanation: Liberalisation removed many trade restrictions.

questions.question: Why do consumers prefer global brands?
questions.explanation: Global brands offer variety and quality.

questions.question: Which policy protects domestic industries from foreign competition?
questions.explanation: Tariffs increase cost of imports and protect domestic firms.

questions.question: Balanced globalisation requires:

options.B: Benefits for all sections of society

questions.explanation: Globalisation should benefit workers, producers and consumers.
